overview.brief_description: San Sebastián is a stunning coastal city in Spain’s Basque Country, renowned for its beautiful beaches, vibrant culinary scene, and rich cultural heritage. Its elegant Belle Époque architecture blends seamlessly with the dramatic backdrop of the Bay of Biscay, making it a captivating destination for travelers seeking both relaxation and sophistication.
why_visit: World-class gastronomy, including numerous Michelin-starred restaurants
Beautiful urban beaches like La Concha and Ondarreta
Rich Basque culture and historic Old Town
overview.best_time_to_visit: May to September, when the weather is warm and festivals are in full swing

seasonal_notes.spring: Mild temperatures and blooming gardens create a pleasant atmosphere, ideal for sightseeing and outdoor dining.
seasonal_notes.summer: Warm and sunny with lively beaches and numerous cultural events, but can be crowded with tourists.
seasonal_notes.autumn: Cooler weather with fewer crowds; perfect for exploring the city’s culinary delights and hiking nearby trails.
seasonal_notes.winter: Generally mild and quieter, offering a peaceful experience with cozy pintxo bars and fewer tourists.

practical_info.getting_around: Walkable city center, supplemented by local buses and taxis
practical_info.safety: Generally very safe, with usual precautions for urban areas

must_do: Stroll along La Concha Beach and enjoy panoramic bay views
Explore the Old Town (Parte Vieja) and sample traditional pintxos
Visit Monte Igueldo for stunning city and coastline vistas
Dine at a Michelin-starred restaurant like Arzak or Akelarre
Attend the San Sebastián International Film Festival if visiting in September
worth_doing: Discover the Basque Culinary Center and learn about local cuisine
Relax at Ondarreta Beach and visit the nearby Peine del Viento sculptures
Take a boat trip across the bay to Santa Clara Island
Visit the San Telmo Museoa to explore Basque history and art
Cycle or walk the scenic Paseo Nuevo along the coast
hidden_gems: Hike up to Mount Urgull and explore the lesser-known historical fortifications
Explore the tranquil neighborhood of Gros with its surf culture and local cafés
Visit the Mercado de la Bretxa for a genuine local market experience
Discover the small but charming Cristina Enea Park away from tourist crowds
Attend a Basque pelota match for a unique cultural sporting experience

sites.name: La Concha Beach
sites.description: La Concha Beach is one of Europe's most famous urban beaches, known for its golden sand and calm bay waters framed by the iconic shell-shaped bay. It's perfect for swimming, sunbathing, and scenic strolls along the promenade with views of Santa Clara Island.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best visited in late spring to early autumn for warm weather and swimming

sites.name: Parte Vieja (Old Town)
sites.description: Parte Vieja is the vibrant historic quarter of San Sebastián, featuring narrow streets lined with pintxo bars, traditional shops, and historic buildings like the Basilica of Saint Mary of the Chorus. It's the heart of the city's culinary and nightlife scene.
sites.seasonal_notes: Lively year-round; especially busy during festivals like Semana Grande

sites.name: Monte Urgull
sites.description: Monte Urgull is a historic hill offering panoramic views over San Sebastián and the Bay of Biscay. The summit hosts the Castillo de la Mota fortress and the iconic Christ statue, blending natural beauty with centuries of history dating back to the 12th century.
sites.seasonal_notes: Lush greenery in spring and summer enhances the experience

sites.name: San Telmo Museoa
sites.description: Housed in a 16th-century former convent, San Telmo Museoa is the main Basque museum showcasing regional history, art, and culture with a modern extension blending historic and contemporary architecture. Exhibits range from archaeological finds to contemporary Basque art.
sites.booking_tips: Booking online is recommended during weekends and holidays

sites.name: Peine del Viento (Comb of the Wind)
sites.description: Peine del Viento is a famous set of three steel sculptures by Eduardo Chillida embedded into the rocks at the western end of Ondarreta Beach. The site offers dramatic views of the waves crashing against the sculptures, blending art with nature.
sites.seasonal_notes: Best experienced during high tide or stormy weather for dramatic wave action

sites.name: Miramar Palace
sites.description: Miramar Palace is a 19th-century royal summer residence set on a hill overlooking La Concha Bay. Visitors can explore its beautiful English-style gardens and enjoy spectacular views of the bay and city.
sites.seasonal_notes: Gardens are best visited in spring and summer when flowers bloom

sites.name: Aquarium Donostia-San Sebastián
sites.description: The Aquarium is a family-friendly attraction featuring marine life native to the Bay of Biscay and beyond. It includes a large oceanarium, interactive exhibits, and a glass tunnel offering immersive underwater views.
sites.booking_tips: Buy tickets online to avoid queues, especially in summer

sites.name: Mercado de la Bretxa
sites.description: Mercado de la Bretxa is a bustling traditional market located near the Old Town, offering fresh local produce, seafood, and Basque specialties. It's a great place to experience the local food culture and buy gourmet ingredients.
sites.seasonal_notes: Seasonal seafood and produce vary throughout the year

sites.name: Basilica of Saint Mary of the Chorus
sites.description: This 18th-century baroque church is a stunning architectural jewel in the Old Town, featuring an ornate façade and richly decorated interiors. It holds an important place in San Sebastián's religious and cultural history.
sites.booking_tips: Check for mass times if interested in attending

sites.name: Tabakalera
sites.description: Tabakalera is a contemporary culture center housed in a former tobacco factory, hosting art exhibitions, film screenings, and creative workshops. It is a hub for modern Basque and international culture and creativity.
sites.booking_tips: Check website for current exhibitions and events

sites.name: Ondarreta Beach
sites.description: Ondarreta Beach is a family-friendly urban beach west of La Concha, known for its fine sand and calm waters, ideal for swimming and sunbathing. It offers a quieter alternative with views of Monte Igueldo and the iconic Peine del Viento sculptures nearby.
sites.seasonal_notes: Peak season from June to September

sites.name: Monte Igueldo
sites.description: Monte Igueldo offers some of the best views over San Sebastián and its coastline. At the summit is a nostalgic amusement park with vintage rides and a historic funicular railway, making it a charming excursion spot.
sites.price_range: Budget to Moderate (for funicular and park entrance)
sites.seasonal_notes: Amusement park open mainly from spring to early autumn
sites.booking_tips: Buy funicular tickets onsite; queues can form on weekends

sites.name: Cristina Enea Park
sites.description: Cristina Enea Park is a peaceful green oasis in the city, featuring walking paths, ponds, and diverse tree species. It’s ideal for a relaxing stroll or picnic and is home to the Cristina Enea Foundation which promotes environmental education.
sites.seasonal_notes: Beautiful in spring and autumn with colorful foliage

sites.name: Museo Chillida-Leku
sites.description: Located just outside San Sebastián, Museo Chillida-Leku is dedicated to the works of Eduardo Chillida, one of Spain’s greatest sculptors. The museum integrates large-scale sculptures with natural landscapes, creating a unique outdoor and indoor art experience.
sites.seasonal_notes: Open seasonally, check opening times in winter
sites.booking_tips: Advance booking recommended, shuttle buses available from San Sebastián

sites.name: Tabakalera Rooftop Terrace
sites.description: The rooftop terrace of Tabakalera offers a fantastic panoramic view of San Sebastián’s cityscape, including the bay, beaches, and mountains. It's a quiet spot to relax and take photos, often overlooked by tourists.
sites.booking_tips: Access included with Tabakalera entry; check opening hours

sites.name: Kursaal Congress Centre and Auditorium
sites.description: Designed by architect Rafael Moneo, the Kursaal is an iconic modern building on the waterfront that hosts concerts, film festivals, and cultural events. Its striking glass cubes contrast with the historic city and provide great photo opportunities.
sites.seasonal_notes: Hosts San Sebastián International Film Festival every September
sites.booking_tips: Book event tickets in advance especially during festivals

neighborhoods.name: Parte Vieja
neighborhoods.character: Bustling and vibrant, Parte Vieja is the historic heart of San Sebastián, alive with pintxo bars and narrow medieval streets. The neighborhood pulses with energy day and night, blending tradition with lively social scenes.

location.description: Located at the western edge of the city center along the bay

history.overview: Parte Vieja is the original medieval core of San Sebastián, dating back to the 12th century.
history.significance: It preserves the city's historic layout and is the birthplace of its renowned culinary culture.

transit: Close to city center bus stops
practical_info.safety: Generally safe but crowded at night; stay alert
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Late afternoon through evening for pintxos and nightlife

attractions.name: Plaza de la Constitución
attractions.description: A lively square that once served as the bullfighting ring in the 19th century.
attractions.appeal: Experience local festivals and vibrant street life.

attractions.name: La Bretxa Market
attractions.description: Traditional market offering fresh produce, seafood, and local delicacies.
attractions.appeal: Ideal for tasting local ingredients and shopping.

dining.name: Bar Nestor
dining.known_for: Famous for its steak and tomato salad

dining.name: Borda Berri
dining.known_for: Creative pintxos with a modern twist

insider_tips: Arrive early evening to beat the crowds at popular pintxo bars.
Explore side streets for quieter bars with local flavor.
Try the anchovies and tortilla pintxos at different bars.

neighborhoods.name: Gros
neighborhoods.character: A trendy and youthful neighborhood, Gros is known for its surf culture and vibrant street art. It blends a laid-back beach vibe with urban energy, attracting creatives and young crowds.

location.description: East of the city center, adjacent to Zurriola Beach

history.overview: Once a working-class area, Gros has transformed into a cultural hotspot since the 2000s.
history.significance: It represents modern San Sebastián’s youthful and artistic side.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring through autumn for beach and festivals
practical_info.walkability: Very walkable with flat streets and bike lanes

attractions.name: Zurriola Beach
attractions.description: Popular surf beach with consistent waves and a youthful crowd.
attractions.appeal: A hub for surfing and beach activities.

attractions.name: Tabakalera
attractions.description: International contemporary culture center housed in a former tobacco factory.
attractions.appeal: Spot for exhibitions, film screenings, and events.

insider_tips: Rent a surfboard or take a lesson at Zurriola Beach.
Visit Tabakalera on Wednesdays for local art markets.
Explore the side streets for street murals and independent shops.

neighborhoods.name: Centro
neighborhoods.character: The bustling commercial and administrative core of San Sebastián, Centro is where elegant boulevards meet high-end shops and historic theaters. It balances modern urban life with refined Belle Époque charm.

location.description: Heart of the city, between Parte Vieja and Amara

history.overview: Developed extensively in the 19th century to accommodate the city's growth and tourism boom.
history.significance: It represents the city’s expansion beyond its medieval core into a modern European city.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Year-round, especially during the San Sebastián Film Festival (September)
practical_info.walkability: Extremely walkable with wide streets and promenades

attractions.name: Victoria Eugenia Theatre
attractions.description: A Belle Époque venue hosting opera, concerts, and cultural events.
attractions.appeal: Architectural beauty and rich cultural programming.

attractions.name: Boulevard
attractions.description: Elegant shopping street lined with boutiques and cafes.
attractions.appeal: Ideal for strolling and upscale shopping.

dining.name: Café de la Concha
dining.known_for: Coffee and pastries with views of La Concha Bay

insider_tips: Visit early morning to enjoy quiet streets and fresh pastries.
Check event schedules at Victoria Eugenia for unique cultural experiences.
Explore side alleys for hidden boutique shops.

neighborhoods.name: Amara
neighborhoods.character: A primarily residential and family-oriented neighborhood, Amara offers a peaceful atmosphere with parks and local markets. It provides a glimpse into everyday life away from the tourist crowds.

location.description: South and slightly west of the city center, inland from the bay

history.overview: Developed mostly in the 20th century as the city expanded beyond its historic core.
history.significance: Key residential area supporting the city’s working and middle class.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and summer for park activities and sports events
practical_info.walkability: Moderately walkable; some areas suited to biking

attractions.name: Anoeta Stadium
attractions.description: Home stadium of Real Sociedad football club.
attractions.appeal: Catch a lively local football match.

attractions.name: Amara Park
attractions.description: A green space with playgrounds and walking paths.
attractions.appeal: A peaceful spot for relaxation and family outings.

insider_tips: Visit on market days to experience local shopping.
Attend a football match for an authentic local experience.
Stroll through Amara Park in the late afternoon.

neighborhoods.name: Antiguo
neighborhoods.character: A charming neighborhood that mixes seaside elegance with residential tranquility, Antiguo is perfect for leisurely strolls and beach access. It offers a more refined, quiet vibe compared to the bustling center.

location.description: West of the city center along Ondarreta Beach

history.overview: Historically a seaside resort area favored by San Sebastián’s aristocracy in the 19th century.
history.significance: Preserves Belle Époque villas and green spaces emblematic of the city’s leisure culture.

transit: Easy access to city center by bus or bike
practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Summer for beach activities and gardens

attractions.name: Miramar Palace
attractions.description: Former royal summer residence with beautiful gardens and bay views.
attractions.appeal: Offers cultural events and panoramic scenery.

attractions.name: Cristina Enea Park
attractions.description: A lush urban park with walking trails and botanical diversity.
attractions.appeal: Perfect for nature walks and picnics.

dining.name: La Perla
dining.known_for: Traditional Basque dishes near the beach

insider_tips: Walk the coastal path from Ondarreta to La Concha beach for stunning views.
Visit Miramar Palace gardens early morning for tranquility.
Try seafood specialties at Elkano with advance reservation.

neighborhoods.name: Egia
neighborhoods.character: An up-and-coming neighborhood with a creative spirit, Egia blends old industrial roots with modern cultural venues and cafes. It appeals to locals and visitors seeking a less touristy, authentic urban experience.

location.description: South of the city center, along the Urumea River

history.overview: Historically a working-class district with factories and warehouses, now undergoing cultural revitalization.
history.significance: Represents San Sebastián’s evolving urban identity and creative economy.

practical_info.best_time_to_visit: Spring and autumn for cultural events

attractions.name: Tabakalera
attractions.description: A hub for contemporary arts, including exhibitions and film screenings.
attractions.appeal: Cutting-edge cultural programming in a former tobacco factory.

attractions.name: Egia Park
attractions.description: Small green space with playgrounds and seating areas.
attractions.appeal: A quiet spot popular with locals.

insider_tips: Check Tabakalera’s calendar for unique exhibitions and workshops.
Explore local street art on quieter side streets.
Try craft beers paired with creative pintxos at Casa 887.

fine_dining.name: Arzak
fine_dining.description: A three-Michelin-starred temple of innovative Basque cuisine led by Juan Mari Arzak and daughter Elena.
fine_dining.local_tips: Book months in advance and try the tasting menu to experience the full creativity.

fine_dining.name: Mugaritz
fine_dining.description: Renowned for its experimental dishes and artful presentation, Mugaritz offers a sensory culinary journey.
fine_dining.local_tips: Allow plenty of time; multi-course tasting menus can last over 3 hours.

fine_dining.name: Akelaŕe
fine_dining.description: Three Michelin stars and spectacular views over La Concha Bay complement Chef Pedro Subijana’s refined Basque dishes.
fine_dining.local_tips: Try the tasting menu paired with local txakoli wine.

casual_dining.name: Bodegón Alejandro
casual_dining.description: Family-run restaurant serving classic Basque dishes with fresh, seasonal ingredients in a cozy setting.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try the lamb shoulder and the local cider.

casual_dining.name: La Cuchara de San Telmo
casual_dining.description: Popular spot for innovative pintxos combining traditional flavors with modern twists.
casual_dining.local_tips: Arrive early or late to avoid long waits; great for sharing small dishes.

casual_dining.name: Gandarias Jatetxea
casual_dining.description: Classic Basque grill house known for excellent steaks and a wide pintxos selection.
casual_dining.local_tips: Try the chuleta (steak) and reserve ahead for dinner.

casual_dining.name: Bar Nestor
casual_dining.description: Small and famous for its tomato salad, chuleta steak, and tortilla de patatas.
casual_dining.local_tips: Only open for lunch; get there early before they sell out.

casual_dining.name: Kokotxa
casual_dining.description: Michelin-starred restaurant offering creative takes on Basque classics in a relaxed environment.
casual_dining.local_tips: Opt for the tasting menu with wine pairings.

street_food.name: La Bretxa Market
street_food.description: Historic market offering fresh seafood, local produce, and pintxos stalls for quick bites.
street_food.local_tips: Try fresh anchovies and local cheeses from vendors.

street_food.name: Pintxos at Calle 31 de Agosto
street_food.description: Famous street lined with pintxos bars offering a variety of bite-sized Basque delicacies.
street_food.local_tips: Try a variety of pintxos and pair with local cider or txakoli wine.

street_food.name: Mercado de San Martín
street_food.description: Cozy market with stalls selling fresh produce and local snacks, ideal for sampling Basque flavors.
street_food.local_tips: Sample local olives and freshly baked txistorra sausages.

bars.name: Bar Txepetxa
bars.description: Specializes in anchovy-based pintxos with unique sauces and toppings.
bars.local_tips: Try the anchovy with green pepper and walnut sauce.

bars.name: Dabadaba
bars.description: Hip bar known for live music, craft beers, and a youthful atmosphere.
bars.local_tips: Check their event schedule for local bands.

bars.name: La Gintonería
bars.description: Elegant bar offering a wide selection of gins and expertly crafted cocktails.
bars.local_tips: Try their signature gin cocktails infused with local herbs.

bars.name: Borda Berri
bars.description: Popular spot for innovative pintxos paired with local wines and sidra.
bars.local_tips: Try the risotto with Idiazabal cheese.

cafes.name: Café de la Concha
cafes.description: Classic café with stunning views of La Concha beach, perfect for coffee and pastries.
cafes.local_tips: Try the Basque cheesecake with your coffee.

cafes.name: Pastelería Otaegui
cafes.description: Historic bakery known for traditional Basque cakes and pastries, including pantxineta.
cafes.local_tips: Try the pantxineta, a puff pastry dessert filled with custard.

cafes.name: Old Town Coffee
cafes.description: Modern café focusing on quality beans and artisanal brewing techniques.

cafes.name: Bergara
cafes.description: Charming café offering brunch, cakes, and excellent coffee with a local vibe.
cafes.local_tips: Try the homemade carrot cake and freshly squeezed juices.

food_experiences.name: San Sebastián Pintxos Tour
food_experiences.description: Guided walking tour exploring the best pintxos bars in the Old Town with tastings at multiple stops.
food_experiences.local_tips: Wear comfortable shoes and bring an appetite; pacing is relaxed but you’ll sample a lot.

food_experiences.name: Basque Cooking Class
food_experiences.description: Hands-on class to learn traditional Basque recipes like marmitako and bacalao pil-pil.
food_experiences.local_tips: Ideal for home chefs wanting a deep dive into Basque culinary culture.

food_experiences.name: La Bretxa Market Visit with Chef
food_experiences.description: Morning tour of La Bretxa market with a local chef, followed by a cooking demonstration.
food_experiences.local_tips: Great for understanding ingredient sourcing and seasonal produce.

food_experiences.name: Txakoli Wine Tasting Experience
food_experiences.description: Visit nearby Txakoli wineries with tastings of this crisp, local Basque white wine paired with pintxos.
food_experiences.local_tips: Combine with a scenic drive along the Basque coast.

spring: Asparagus from Navarra – fresh, tender, often served grilled or in omelets
Mushrooms – wild varieties appear in markets and restaurants
Txangurro – spider crab dishes are popular as the crab season peaks
summer: Txakoli – young, crisp Basque white wine ideal for hot days
Piquillo Peppers – roasted and stuffed, a light seasonal treat
Seafood – fresh anchovies, sardines, and shellfish at their peak
autumn: Mushrooms – chanterelles and other wild fungi feature prominently
Game meats – venison and wild boar appear on menus
Chestnuts – roasted and used in desserts and savory dishes
winter: Marmitako – hearty tuna and potato stew, perfect for cold days
Idiazabal Cheese – smoked sheep’s milk cheese often enjoyed with cider
Cider (Sagardoa) – traditional Basque cider houses open for tastings

transport_types.notes: Direct Alvia trains from San Sebastian to Madrid Chamartín station.
Comfortable direct buses departing multiple times daily.
One transfer typically in Zaragoza; fast connections available.
Direct buses available; overnight options possible.
High-speed TGV from San Sebastian to Paris Gare de Lyon via Hendaye.
Flights depart from San Sebastian Airport to Paris Charles de Gaulle or Orly.
Frequent regional trains linking San Sebastian and Bilbao.
Regular bus services complement train connections.
Direct regional trains via Hendaye; connects with TGV services.
Affordable bus options with several departures daily.
Connecting flights via Madrid or Barcelona common; some direct charters.
Direct flights from San Sebastian Airport; limited daily frequency.
Flights to London Heathrow and Gatwick from San Sebastian Airport.
Usually requires connection in Barcelona or Madrid; no direct flights.
Connecting flights via Barcelona or Madrid; no direct flights.

Spring.name: Tamborrada Festival
Spring.description: A vibrant 24-hour drum parade celebrating San Sebastián’s patron saint with locals dressed in traditional costumes.

Spring.name: Pintxo Tasting in Old Town
Spring.description: Enjoy sampling a variety of pintxos, the local Basque tapas, in the historic Parte Vieja area.

Spring.name: Stroll Along La Concha Beach
Spring.description: Take a refreshing walk on the famous crescent-shaped beach as spring temperatures begin to rise.

Spring.name: San Telmo Museoa Visit
Spring.description: Explore Basque history, art, and culture in this museum housed in a 16th-century convent.

Summer.name: San Sebastián International Film Festival
Summer.description: Attend screenings and events at one of Europe’s most prestigious film festivals held annually in September.

Summer.name: Surfing at Zurriola Beach
Summer.description: Catch waves or take lessons at this popular surf spot known for its consistent breaks.

Summer.name: Open-Air Jazzaldia Festival
Summer.description: Enjoy free and ticketed jazz concerts on stages throughout the city during this renowned summer festival.

Summer.name: Boat Trip to Santa Clara Island
Summer.description: Take a short ferry ride to this small island for swimming, sunbathing, and picnicking.

Summer.name: Sampling Txakoli Wine
Summer.description: Taste the local slightly sparkling white wine from nearby vineyards, perfect for summer refreshment.

Autumn.name: Cider House Visits (Sagardotegiak)
Autumn.description: Experience traditional Basque cider houses with tastings and hearty seasonal meals.

Autumn.name: Hiking Monte Urgull
Autumn.description: Enjoy cooler weather while hiking this historic hill offering panoramic views over the city and bay.

Autumn.name: San Sebastián Gastronomika Conference
Autumn.description: Attend one of the world’s leading gastronomic congresses featuring top chefs and culinary innovations.

Autumn.name: Visit Aquarium and Sealife Centre
Autumn.description: Explore marine biodiversity through interactive exhibits suitable for all ages.

Autumn.name: Autumn Pintxo Routes
Autumn.description: Try seasonal pintxos featuring autumn ingredients like mushrooms and chestnuts in local bars.

Winter.name: Christmas Market at Plaza de la Constitución
Winter.description: Browse festive stalls offering crafts, gifts, and seasonal treats in the historic city square.

Winter.name: Enjoy Basque Hot Chocolate and Churros
Winter.description: Warm up with traditional thick hot chocolate served with crispy churros at local cafés.

Winter.name: Visit the San Telmo Museoa
Winter.description: Escape the cold by exploring Basque arts and history inside this atmospheric museum.

Winter.name: Winter Walks on Ondarreta Beach
Winter.description: Take brisk walks along this quieter beach, enjoying the fresh sea air and views of Mount Igueldo.

Winter.name: Kursaal Auditorium Concerts
Winter.description: Attend winter concerts ranging from classical music to jazz at this iconic modern venue.

ranges.notes: Cool and wet weather, low tourist crowds except for Epiphany celebrations on Jan 6, a charming local festival.
Cold, often rainy weather with low tourist activity; good for budget travelers seeking deals but limited outdoor activities.
Mid to late January continues cold, damp weather; very low crowds and off-season prices but limited daylight and outdoor options.
Cold and rainy with some wind; low tourist numbers and good prices, but outdoor activities are limited and daylight is short.
Carnival week brings vibrant street events and cultural activities; weather still cool but crowds increase moderately during celebrations.
Post-carnival low season with fewer tourists; weather remains cool but signs of spring appear; good for walking and city exploration.
Early March sees improving weather with occasional rain; low tourist density; ideal for budget travelers and those seeking quiet visits.

ranges.event: Tamborrada de San Sebastián (Jan 20 postponed celebrations or local events in March sometimes)
ranges.notes: In some years, local events or cultural exhibitions occur; weather starts warming; moderate crowds as tourism picks up.
Late March offers mild weather, increasing sunshine, and fewer crowds before Easter; great for outdoor activities and cultural visits.
Spring weather brings moderate temperatures and occasional showers; crowds growing but still manageable; good for sightseeing and gastronomy.
Easter week features religious processions and local festivities; weather is mild; crowds increase especially around holidays.
Post-Easter, pleasant temperatures and blooming landscapes; moderate crowds; perfect for beach walks and city exploration.
May offers some of the best weather with warm temperatures, low rainfall, and blossoming nature; perfect for beach and outdoor activities.
Late May is ideal with longer days, pleasant sea temperatures, and vibrant city life; crowd levels increase but remain comfortable.
Early June continues excellent weather with warm, sunny days and cool evenings; great for beach time and outdoor festivals beginning.

ranges.event: San Sebastián International Film Festival (late June some years)
ranges.notes: Festival attracts many visitors; excellent weather; expect higher crowds and prices but unique cultural experiences.
July is warm with long sunny days; beach season is in full swing; high crowds and prices but vibrant city atmosphere.
Mid to late July maintains hot weather and crowded beaches; festivals and nightlife lively; good for social and luxury travelers.
August is peak summer with hot weather and maximum crowds; beach activities dominate; expect high prices and busy restaurants.
San Sebastián's largest festival with fireworks, concerts, and cultural events; extremely crowded but unique vibrant experience; warm and sunny weather.
Early September enjoys warm weather with less humidity and smaller crowds than August; ideal for beach and city tours.
Late September sees gradual cooling and increasing rainfall; fewer tourists; good for cultural visits and gastronomy.
October brings cooler, wetter weather; off-peak season with lower prices but less ideal for beach; good for museum visits and culinary tourism.
Mid to late October has variable weather with rain and winds; low tourist numbers and affordable rates; good for quiet city vacations.
November is cool, rainy, and windy; low season with very few tourists; some indoor cultural events but limited outdoor appeal.
Late November continues cold and wet patterns; very quiet city with discounted accommodation but limited events and outdoor options.
Early December has cool weather and increasing festive decorations; moderate crowds for holiday shopping and cultural events.
Christmas markets and festive atmosphere pick up; cold but charming; increased visitors but manageable crowds.
Holiday celebrations with special events and dinners; cold weather; high demand for accommodation; festive and lively but crowded.
